meta.scene_summary: We are busy building the future with nuclear power and AI agents while the current version of the internet is busy being broken or held for ransom.

cooking.headline: Meta is buying enough nuclear power to make your utility bill feel personally attacked
cooking.summary: Meta is partnering with TerraPower to build eight nuclear reactors just to keep their data centers running. It's comforting to know that while your local grid struggles to handle a microwave and a hairdryer, the metaverse is powered by its own private Manhattan Project.
cooking.plain_english: Meta has signed an agreement with TerraPower to construct eight small modular nuclear reactors to provide electricity for its data centers.

cooking.headline: Project Valhalla has been in development longer than some of your interns have been alive.
cooking.summary: Java's most anticipated update is finally arriving after a decade of waiting, proving that enterprise software development runs on geological timescales. It is essentially the 'we have food at home' of language features, just served ten years later.
cooking.plain_english: The Java programming language is introducing a significant update that improves how data is stored and managed in memory to increase performance.

cooking.headline: TypeScript 7.0 is here, because your type definitions weren't complex enough already.
cooking.summary: Microsoft is pushing out another major update that promises to make your codebase more robust while simultaneously making your build times feel like a hostage situation. It is the perfect excuse to spend your entire sprint refactoring code that was working perfectly fine.
cooking.plain_english: Microsoft has released a pre-release version of TypeScript 7.0, which includes new features and improvements for the programming language.

cooking.headline: AI agents are now doing the QA work you definitely forgot to finish
cooking.summary: TesterArmy is launching AI agents to handle app testing, effectively outsourcing the most tedious part of your release cycle. It's the digital equivalent of hiring a group project member who actually shows up to do the work.
cooking.plain_english: TesterArmy has released automated software agents that test web and mobile applications for bugs and functionality issues.

cooking.headline: Godot 4.7 just dropped and it's still the engine that refuses to sell its soul
cooking.summary: Godot keeps shipping updates without forcing a subscription model or a soul-crushing EULA down your throat. It turns out you don't actually need to pay a licensing tax to make a game that runs better than your current project.
cooking.plain_english: The latest version of the free, open-source Godot game engine has been released with new features and improvements.

cooked.headline: GitHub is currently hosting a 10,000-repo malware party nobody wanted
cooked.summary: Someone found 10,000 repositories distributing Trojan malware, proving once again that 'git clone' is the digital equivalent of eating food off a subway floor. At this point, I'm not sure if GitHub is a code repository or just a very elaborate distribution network for people who hate their own OS.
cooked.plain_english: A security researcher identified 10,000 GitHub repositories that were intentionally distributing Trojan malware to users.

cooked.headline: Let's Encrypt had a rough morning, and so did every HTTPS site using it
cooked.summary: The internet's favorite free security blanket hit a snag during certificate renewals today. It turns out that when the automated handshake fails, the entire web feels like it's being held together by a single piece of scotch tape.
cooked.plain_english: Let's Encrypt experienced technical issues that prevented many websites from automatically renewing their security certificates.

cooked.headline: Amazon is investigating employees who pointed out the irony of their AI climate impact
cooked.summary: Amazon is reportedly digging into staff who questioned the environmental cost of building more AI data centers. Nothing says 'forward-thinking tech giant' quite like launching a corporate investigation into people who read the company's own sustainability reports.
cooked.plain_english: Amazon is investigating employees who publicly criticized the company's expansion of data centers used for artificial intelligence.

cooked.headline: SK Telecom is learning that global AI expansion comes with a side of geopolitical red tape
cooked.summary: SK Telecom is caught in the crossfire of export controls while trying to help Anthropic build a global AI platform. It turns out that selling frontier tech across borders is a lot harder than just pushing to production.
cooked.plain_english: SK Telecom is facing regulatory scrutiny regarding its partnership with Anthropic due to international export control laws on advanced artificial intelligence technology.

cooked.headline: Elkjop learned the hard way that 'forced consent' is just a fancy term for a €1.8M invoice
cooked.summary: A privacy advocate spent five years warning the company that their consent practices were illegal, only for them to ignore it until the regulators showed up. It turns out that 'ignoring the developer' is a great way to speedrun a massive regulatory fine.
cooked.plain_english: The retail company Elkjop was fined 1.8 million euros for using unlawful methods to obtain customer consent for data processing.
